heading: Postage and revenue stamps of foreign governments
label: 502 Postage and revenue stamps of foreign governments
text: Whoever forges, or counterfeits, or knowingly utters or uses any forged or counterfeit postage stamp or revenue stamp of any foreign government, shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than five years, or both.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 349 (, ; , ).Mar. 4, 1909, ch. 321, § 22035 Stat. 1132May 26, 1926, ch. 39644 Stat. 653A paragraph defining “foreign government” was combined with other like provisions to form . A proviso against repeal, “Provided, however, That nothing in this section shall be held to repeal or modify [now ]”, was deleted as unnecessary since that section by express reference to this one makes it clear that these sections are in pari materia.section 11 of this titlesection 350 of this titlesection 504 of this titleMinor changes in phraseology were also made.
Amendments1994— substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $500”.Pub. L. 103–322
